title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL SQL builder accepts duplicate idempotency key columns
summary: The Oracle MySQL SQL builder accepted duplicate idempotency key column entries.
observed: A key column list could contain the same mapped column more than once.
expected: Idempotency key columns must be unique.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for duplicate idempotency key columns.
impact: Duplicate key entries make the idempotency policy harder to review and can hide copy-and-paste mistakes.
delivery_semantics: The fix preserves existing delivery behavior while rejecting ambiguous idempotency configuration.
security: Explicit duplicate rejection supports auditable replay and duplicate-handling policy.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL idempotency key columns reject duplicates.
Valid unique key column lists remain accepted.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL SQL tests.
documentation: Document that idempotency columns must be unique.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the SQL validation fix is published.
